sections.title: A: Multiple Choice (The Age of Exploration)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).
sections.passage:
The age of exploration (1) .......... a lasting difference to our understanding of the world, and many historians see it as the beginning of the modern (2) .......... age. Ancient sea charts and maps were (3) .......... in libraries across Europe, showing that early sailors knew more about distant lands than many had believed. The (4) .......... around the Mediterranean Sea played a key role in the earliest voyages of discovery. The (5) .......... Greeks and Phoenicians had already crossed vast stretches of ocean long before the explorers of the fifteenth century set sail. It is (6) .......... that Christopher Columbus never accepted that he had reached a new continent. The population of the Americas at the time of European arrival was (7) .......... at around sixty million people. The (8) .......... world map — one that included all the major continents — was not produced until the early sixteenth century. Interest in exploration (9) .......... to countries across Europe as news of new discoveries reached royal courts and trading companies. Major changes took (10) .......... in trade, science, and politics as a direct result of these voyages. As time (11) .......... , improvements in navigation made longer journeys increasingly possible. Many of the explorers who made the most significant discoveries remained (12) .......... to ordinary people for hundreds of years.

If we (13) .......... the early history of exploration more carefully, we find many remarkable stories of ambition and courage. Sailors used the stars in (14) .......... to calculate their exact position at sea. Early mapmakers placed dotted lines in (15) .......... of firm borders to indicate territories that were still uncertain.

sections.title: B: Multiple Choice (Vocabulary)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: The museum exhibit was impressive, but visitors noticed that the ...... gemstones used in the display were not real.
The ...... between the two business partners has always been built on trust and mutual respect.
Drivers caught using their phones while at the wheel will be .......
The new ice cream shop that opened last month has quickly become very ...... with local students.
I picked up the newspaper and saw that the ...... read 'New Hospital to Open Next Year'.
If you don't leave soon, you'll ...... the last train back to the city.
The professional footballer required ...... on his knee after the serious injury he suffered during the match.
The defence lawyer asked the ...... to confirm that she had clearly seen the defendant leaving the building.
Basketball is a ...... that develops both physical fitness and teamwork skills.
The local politician was ...... with misuse of public funds following a lengthy investigation.

sections.title: C: Open Cloze (Getting into Photography)
sections.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
sections.passage:
Many people look at (1) .......... stunning photographs shared online and become inspired by the art of capturing the world around them. (2) .......... that sounds like you, then perhaps it's time you tried your own camera. Photography (3) .......... be a wonderful hobby and we've written this simple guide for those of you thinking of (4) .......... it up.

**What if I don't have much money?**

Start with your smartphone. You can still take beautiful shots, as (5) .......... as you don't expect to produce so (6) .......... technical detail. Explore parks and city streets — and who knows? You might (7) .......... discover a real talent for it! Remember, though, that you (8) .......... always ask permission before photographing people you don't know.

**What kind of camera do I need?**

Depending on what you want to capture, you need to choose (9) .......... a compact and a mirrorless camera. A compact camera with automatic settings is (10) .......... great way of quickly (11) .......... your eye for composition. As well as more interesting subjects (12) .......... you might imagine, there are also creative techniques to explore! You should have no difficulty (13) .......... a style that suits you. If you want to take photography seriously, you (14) .......... need a camera with full manual controls. These cameras are much (15) .......... at giving you creative freedom and allow you to capture truly extraordinary images!

Whatever you decide, welcome to the wonderful world of photography!

sections.title: D: Multiple Choice (Grammar)
sections.instructions: Choose the correct answer (A, B, C, or D).

items.sentence: I think you've been waiting ...... and the manager should see you now.
'Did you reply to her message?' 'No, because I ...... deal with an urgent call. I'll write back later today.'
There ...... be a small bookshop on this corner before the supermarket took it over.
If the weather had been better, we ...... a lovely picnic in the park.
'Why are you taking your charger?' 'Oh, just ...... my laptop battery runs out.'
I suppose we really ...... to book a table at the restaurant in advance.
I'm afraid we've run out of ...... to go over all the details today.
The driver might have avoided the accident if she ...... the warning sign in time.
This suitcase is ...... heavy for me to carry up the stairs on my own.
The lecture was held in a large hall, but ...... students actually turned up to attend.
The main investors have decided to pull **F** out of the construction project after costs rose sharply.
The new employee finds it difficult to get **H** on with the rest of the team.
After reviewing all the applications, we've managed to narrow it **A** down to four strong candidates.
The committee agreed to look **B** into the noise complaints raised by local residents.
It appears that Sophie has fallen **D** out with her sister over a minor disagreement.
It was Elena who came **C** up with the idea for the charity fundraising event.
The exhibition started quietly but turned **G** out to be one of the most visited of the year.

sections.title: F: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
I lost my wallet once before this week. **second**
This is the ...... my wallet this week.

I took up playing squash six years ago. **for**
I ...... six years.

I can't wait until I'm old enough to travel abroad alone. **forward**
I'm really ...... old enough to travel abroad alone.

Speeding on wet roads often causes serious accidents. **results**
Speeding on wet roads ...... serious accidents.

It's pointless denying that you were there because three people saw you. **point**
There ...... you were there because three people saw you.

Don't you wish you could travel around the world? **able**
Wouldn't you love ...... around the world?

I phoned the hotel to confirm that my booking was correct. **make**
I phoned the hotel to ...... my booking was correct.

I'm afraid you're too young to enter this competition. **old**
I'm afraid you ...... to enter this competition.

My father says the city centre wasn't so crowded in the past. **used**
My father says the city centre ...... so crowded.

sections.title: G: Error Correction (Extra word)
sections.instructions: If a line is correct, press OK. If there is an extra word, write it.

items.sentence: I had a brilliant week at school! We were been working on a short film
project when our teacher told to us that our work might be shown at the
local community centre. I was really keen on
getting involved because I've always had been fascinated by a
career in the cinema. She asked us to plan our scenes in
detail and to write a proper script. Most people found it out difficult
to develop original characters, but I didn't. I thought it would be great to
interview local people who were used to attend our school,
talking about how the school had shaped [them] their futures and given them the
confidence to follow their ambitions. My teacher loved the idea and said I should
to contact some former students. I asked the head teacher for
a few contact details and she provided several names. When I reached out and
described them what I was hoping to do, they were all delighted to
take part. I plan to film them at a local café using a small camera and record
precisely that what they tell me. Film-making is quickly becoming my favourite school project!

sections.title: H: Word Formation
sections.instructions: Use the word in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap.
sections.passage: Some (1) .......... are remembered for their daring, while others make decisions so poor that it seems completely (2) .......... . Consider the case of one (3) .......... who broke into a large department store late at night. After searching the aisles for valuable items, he came across an extremely (4) .......... sofa in the furniture section and decided to take a short rest. It may seem (5) .......... , but he was still fast asleep when the first security guard arrived the next morning. The guard (6) .......... called the police and reported the bizarre situation. Another (7) .......... case involved a man who stole a professional (8) .......... camera system from a local office. He managed to carry all the (9) .......... away without being noticed, but had accidentally left his identity card on the reception desk. It was used as (10) .......... against him in court, and he was found guilty within the hour.
